note: The topic glossary. Every article's topics[] must resolve to a slug here. Topics make the archive browseable by humans (/topics) and machines (/topics/<slug>.txt). Aliases route to a canonical slug.

strait-of-hormuz.name: Strait of Hormuz
strait-of-hormuz.blurb: The world's principal oil-and-gas maritime chokepoint, between Iran and the Arabian Peninsula; about a fifth of global petroleum-liquids transit it.

us-iran.name: US–Iran
us-iran.blurb: The diplomatic and military relationship between Washington and Tehran, including the 2026 war and its negotiated endgame.

middle-east-war.name: Middle East War 2026
middle-east-war.blurb: The regional conflict spanning Iran, Israel, Lebanon and the Gulf, and the diplomacy around ending it.

lebanon.name: Lebanon
lebanon.blurb: The Israel–Hezbollah front and Lebanon's contested place in any regional settlement.

maritime-security.name: Maritime Security
maritime-security.blurb: Law, force, and freedom of navigation at sea — chokepoints, inspections, blockades, and coercion on the water.

unclos.name: Law of the Sea
unclos.blurb: The UN Convention on the Law of the Sea — transit passage, innocent passage, and the limits of coastal-state authority.

south-china-sea.name: South China Sea
south-china-sea.blurb: Contested maritime sovereignty around China, Taiwan, the Philippines and Southeast Asia.

grey-zone.name: Grey-Zone Coercion
grey-zone.blurb: Pressure applied below the threshold of declared war — law enforcement, inspections, and administration used as instruments of force.

central-banks.name: Central Banks
central-banks.blurb: Monetary policy and the decisions of the Fed, ECB, BoJ, BoE and their peers.

inflation.name: Inflation
inflation.blurb: Consumer and producer price levels and the forces — energy, wages, supply — behind them.

rates.name: Rates
rates.blurb: Government bond yields, the curve, and the term-premium debate across major jurisdictions.

oil.name: Oil
oil.blurb: Crude oil markets — Brent and WTI — and the geopolitics and logistics that move them.

shipping.name: Shipping
shipping.blurb: Ocean freight, tanker and container rates, and the maritime logistics chain.

war-risk-insurance.name: War-Risk Insurance
war-risk-insurance.blurb: Marine insurance pricing for transits through conflict zones — a real-money read on perceived risk.

critical-minerals.name: Critical Minerals
critical-minerals.blurb: Concentrated materials inputs — rare earths, gallium, germanium, indium — whose supply can be weaponised.

export-controls.name: Export Controls
export-controls.blurb: State restrictions on strategic exports — chips, materials, and the supply chains they gate.

supply-chains.name: Supply Chains
supply-chains.blurb: Global production and logistics dependencies and their chokepoints.

spacex.name: SpaceX
spacex.blurb: The launch-and-satellite company, its Starlink business, and its record public-market debut.

elon-musk.name: Elon Musk
elon-musk.blurb: The entrepreneur behind SpaceX, Tesla, xAI and X, and the concentration of control across them.

space.name: Space
space.blurb: The space economy — launch, satellites, and orbital infrastructure including space-based compute.

ipos.name: IPOs
ipos.blurb: Public-market listings — pricing, demand, and the access split between institutions and retail.

wealth-concentration.name: Wealth Concentration
wealth-concentration.blurb: The distribution of wealth and corporate control, and the politics of extreme fortunes.

ai-compute.name: AI Compute
ai-compute.blurb: The hardware, power, and materials underpinning artificial intelligence at scale.

agentic-commerce.name: Agentic Commerce
agentic-commerce.blurb: AI agents shopping and transacting on a user's behalf — and the liability, identity, and control questions that follow.

ai-agents.name: AI Agents
ai-agents.blurb: Autonomous software agents that act, not just answer — the premise of this paper, now loose in the economy.

payments.name: Payments
payments.blurb: Payment networks and rails — Visa, Mastercard — and their extension into new layers of commerce.

openai.name: OpenAI
openai.blurb: The AI lab behind ChatGPT and its expanding role in commerce and the agentic stack.

creator-economy.name: Creator Economy
creator-economy.blurb: Individual creators as media institutions and economic forces — audience as the new scale.

attention-economy.name: Attention Economy
attention-economy.blurb: The competition for human attention as a measurable, ownable resource.

world-cup.name: World Cup 2026
world-cup.blurb: The expanded FIFA World Cup across the US, Canada and Mexico — and its logistics, security and access politics.

sport.name: Sport
sport.blurb: Athletic competition as news, forecast, and culture.

mega-events.name: Mega-Events
mega-events.blurb: Large-scale global events as stress tests of a host's logistics, security and openness.

us-immigration.name: US Immigration
us-immigration.blurb: US border, visa and travel policy and its second-order effects on commerce, sport and culture.

europe-defense.name: European Defence
europe-defense.blurb: NATO and European security planning, especially as the US redirects forces toward the Pacific.

anthropic.name: Anthropic
anthropic.blurb: The AI lab behind Claude — and, in June 2026, the first whose frontier models were pulled by a US export-control order.

frontier-models.name: Frontier Models
frontier-models.blurb: The most capable AI models at the edge of the field — and the access governance now attached to them.

ai-export-controls.name: AI Export Controls
ai-export-controls.blurb: State restrictions extended from chips to model access — capability-as-a-service treated as controlled technology.

developer-infrastructure.name: Developer Infrastructure
developer-infrastructure.blurb: The APIs, models and platforms software is built on — and the single points of failure they create.

china-ai.name: China AI
china-ai.blurb: China’s AI labs and models — DeepSeek, Zhipu and peers — rising as substitutes amid US controls.

ukraine.name: Ukraine
ukraine.blurb: Russia’s war on Ukraine — the front, the strikes on energy and Black Sea port infrastructure, and the fiscal and FX strain on Kyiv.

food-security.name: Food Security
food-security.blurb: Grain and food-export flows and prices, especially Black Sea corridor disruption.

sudan.name: Sudan
sudan.blurb: Sudan’s civil war between the army (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) — sieges, drone warfare, and mass-atrocity risk across Darfur and Kordofan.

robotics.name: Robotics
robotics.blurb: Embodied AI and general-purpose machines — humanoid and industrial robots, the labs and factories racing to deploy them, and the US-China contest over who builds the body for the model.

autonomous-weapons.name: Autonomous Weapons
autonomous-weapons.blurb: Lethal autonomy and the drone industrial base — loitering munitions, counter-drone systems, autonomy in targeting, and the policy fight over machines that decide.

defense-tech.name: Defense Tech
defense-tech.blurb: The new defense-industrial base — Anduril, Shield AI, Helsing and the software-first contractors rebuilding how war is supplied and fought.

compute.name: Compute
compute.blurb: Compute as territory — datacenter mega-builds, power and grid constraints, chips, and sovereign-compute deals; the physical buildout underneath AI.

taiwan.name: Taiwan
taiwan.blurb: The Taiwan flashpoint — Chinese military pressure around the island, the shrinking warning time, and the global stakes riding on its semiconductor fabs.

semiconductors.name: Semiconductors
semiconductors.blurb: The chip supply chain as contested terrain — TSMC, fabs, advanced-node concentration, and the geopolitics of who makes the silicon AI runs on.

nuclear-power.name: Nuclear Power
nuclear-power.blurb: Atomic energy as AI infrastructure — reactor financing, the power buildout to feed data-center demand, and the grid as a national-security variable.
